title: Desktop IDE
description: Use the Tauri shell for reliable project FS and richer LSP.

steps.caption: Open with Tauri
steps.narration: Launch the desktop app from the desktop folder with npm run dev. Click Open Project — Tauri shows a native folder picker and loads every file under juni.toml, including assets as data URLs when needed.

steps.caption: Save to disk
steps.narration: Edit a scene or Juni source, then Save Scene or save the file tab. Writes go through write_project_file to the real project root — no download fallback when the desktop root is open.

steps.caption: Hover and diagnostics
steps.narration: Desktop Monaco talks to juni-lsp. Hover a symbol for kind and detail. Diagnostics from parse and type-check appear as Monaco markers while you type — beyond thin complete and goto.

steps.caption: External editors
steps.narration: For VS Code or other editors, run juni lsp from the project root. The same workspace provides completion, definition, hover, and pull diagnostics over stdio.
